topic: LangChain vs LlamaIndex 2026: Which RAG Framework Should You Choose?

competitors.title: LangChain vs LlamaIndex (2026) – MorphLLM
key_points: LangGraph better for complex stateful agent workflows with built-in persistence, human-in-the-loop
LlamaIndex better for retrieval-heavy applications with hierarchical chunking, auto-merging, sub-question decomposition
GitHub stars: LangChain 119K vs LlamaIndex 44K
Integrations: LangChain 500+ vs LlamaIndex 300+
RAG abstractions: LangChain component-based (manual assembly) vs LlamaIndex purpose-built
Agent framework: LangGraph (stateful graph) vs LlamaIndex Workflows (event-driven async)
Framework overhead: LangGraph ~14ms vs LlamaIndex ~6ms
Token overhead: LangChain ~2.4K vs LlamaIndex ~1.6K
Code volume for RAG: LangChain requires 30-40% more code
Observability: LangSmith (first-party) vs LlamaIndex third-party integrations
Version stability: LangChain had breaking changes pre-1.0; LlamaIndex more stable
Pricing: Both free core; LangGraph Cloud usage-based; LlamaCloud Pro $500/month

competitors.title: LangChain vs LlamaIndex (2026): Complete Production RAG Comparison – Prem AI
key_points: LangChain has effectively become LangGraph for production; LlamaIndex added Workflows for multi-step agents
Primary identity: LangChain for orchestration+agents vs LlamaIndex for data framework+RAG
Observability: LangSmith (first-party) vs Langfuse/Arize Phoenix integrations
RAG support: LangChain via integrations vs LlamaIndex native, purpose-built
Learning curve: LangChain steeper vs LlamaIndex lower for RAG use cases
Versioning stability: LangChain history of breaking changes vs LlamaIndex more stable upgrade path
Enterprise adoption: LangChain used by Uber, LinkedIn, Replit; LlamaIndex growing in document-heavy verticals
Architecture: LangChain three-layer stack (Core, LangGraph, LangSmith) vs LlamaIndex five abstractions (connectors, parsers, indices, query engines, workflows)
RAG capabilities: LlamaIndex has hierarchical chunking, auto-merging, sub-question decomposition built-in
Agent frameworks: LangGraph stateful graph with persistence vs LlamaIndex Workflows event-driven async
Hybrid stack recommended: LlamaIndex for retrieval + LangGraph for orchestration

stats.fact: LangChain GitHub repository has over 88,000 stars as of October 2024
LangChain weekly downloads hit 15 million across packages
LangChain integrates with 100+ LLM providers and 200+ vector stores
LangGraph agent latency reduced by 40% in tests
LlamaIndex GitHub repository has over 32,000 stars as of October 2024
LlamaIndex npm package downloaded 1.2 million times weekly on average in 2024
65% of Fortune 500 companies use LlamaIndex per 2024 survey
LlamaIndex has 20% market share in open-source RAG frameworks per 2024 analysis
250,000+ monthly active users inferred from PyPI downloads in Q3 2024
LlamaIndex Discord server grew to 25,000 members in 2024

angles: Hybrid approach: Use LlamaIndex for retrieval, LangGraph for orchestration - best of both worlds
Future outlook: Convergence of frameworks with both adding agent workflows and RAG optimizations
Selection criteria: Choose based on primary use case (retrieval vs orchestration) not feature parity
Enterprise readiness: Both have enterprise adoption but differ in observability and stability trade-offs
Cost considerations: Token overhead differences matter at scale; LlamaIndex typically more efficient for pure RAG
outline: Introduction: The convergence of LangChain and LlamaIndex in 2026
Market overview: RAG framework adoption and enterprise trends
Architecture comparison: LangChain/LangGraph stack vs LlamaIndex abstractions
RAG capabilities: Retrieval quality, chunking strategies, and built-in features
Agent frameworks: Stateful graphs vs event-driven workflows
Observability and tooling: LangSmith vs third-party integrations
Performance benchmarks: Latency, token overhead, code volume
Enterprise adoption: Fortune 500 usage and production case studies
Use case recommendations: When to choose each framework
Hybrid approach: Combining LlamaIndex retrieval with LangGraph orchestration
Future trends: GPT-5 integration, multi-modal RAG, and emerging standards
Conclusion: Pragmatic framework selection for 2026 projects
